notes.zh_el_i18n: zh and el name_i18n are 100% absent across all cluster-6 domains (confirmed globally). Not flagged per domain — noted once here. Not an actionable gap for a back-office FR-primary platform.
notes.domain_registry_classification: domain_registry is NOT a ref_code domain/partition. It is a separate table ref_code_domain_registry (schema: domain,name,description,object_type,is_hierarchical,is_taxonomy,position,is_active,name_i18n,description_i18n,metadata,created_at,updated_at) with 19 live rows. Included in scope for completeness; findings treat it as a separate registry, not a ref_code vocabulary.

matrix.db_usage_distinct: Consumed by: api.get_object_taxonomy_compact, api.is_ref_code_taxonomy_domain, api.trg_refresh_ref_code_taxonomy_closure, api.validate_object_taxonomy_assignment, api.validate_ref_code_taxonomy_hierarchy; FE: object-workspace.ts:1129, explorer-reference.ts:357, dashboard-reference.ts:79 — all reading ref_code_domain_registry directly.
matrix.verdict: SEPARATE TABLE — NOT a ref_code partition. ref_code_domain_registry is a standalone registry table (schema differs from ref_code). Live=source=doc=19. Fully consumed. No issue. Scope classification artifact: this is not a ref_code vocabulary.

matrix.db_usage_distinct: api.save_object_openings reads ref_code_opening_schedule_type (doc confirmed). opening_schedule table presumably has a FK (high confidence per FK-4 triggers doc). Active operational vocabulary.
matrix.verdict: ALIGNED — source=live=doc=5. en/es present (5/5). Active consumer confirmed.

matrix.db_usage_distinct: api.save_object_openings reads ref_code_weekday (doc confirmed). opening_time_period_weekday.weekday_id presumably FK. Active operational vocabulary.
matrix.verdict: ALIGNED values — source=live=doc=7. en/es present (7/7). CRITICAL DEFECT: dow_number is NULL for all 7 rows on live. Source seeds_data.sql inserts position (1-7) in the 3rd positional column, NOT dow_number; the i18n block (lines 1221-1227) explicitly passes NULL for dow_number. dow_number was never populated in any seed or migration. See finding D4-01.

findings.title: dow_number NULL for all 7 weekday rows — ISO day-of-week ordering unavailable
findings.evidence: SELECT code, dow_number FROM ref_code WHERE domain='weekday' ORDER BY dow_number returns NULL for all 7 rows. seeds_data.sql lines 78-84 insert (domain,code,name,position) — the 4th column is 'position', not 'dow_number'. The i18n update block (lines 1221-1227) explicitly passes NULL,NULL as the last two arguments (dow_number placeholder). No migration ever sets this column.
findings.recommendation: Add a targeted UPDATE in seeds_data.sql (idempotent, ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING guard not applicable — use WHERE dow_number IS NULL): UPDATE ref_code SET dow_number = CASE code WHEN 'monday' THEN 1 WHEN 'tuesday' THEN 2 WHEN 'wednesday' THEN 3 WHEN 'thursday' THEN 4 WHEN 'friday' THEN 5 WHEN 'saturday' THEN 6 WHEN 'sunday' THEN 7 END WHERE domain='weekday' AND dow_number IS NULL. Convention: use ISO 8601 (Mon=1..Sun=7). Apply as a standalone migration.

findings.title: distribution_channel rows stored in DEFAULT partition (ref_code_other) — no dedicated partition table
findings.evidence: pg_inherits scan shows no ref_code_distribution_channel partition. SELECT count(*) FROM ref_code_other WHERE domain='distribution_channel' returns 4. The 4 rows exist and are accessible but bypass domain-level partition isolation, dedicated index, and per-partition RLS granularity that other ref_code domains receive.
findings.recommendation: Create ref_code_distribution_channel PARTITION OF ref_code FOR VALUES IN ('distribution_channel') with the standard uq_ref_code_distribution_channel_id and uq_ref_code_distribution_channel_code unique indexes, RLS enabled, and the standard pub_ref_code_read / admin_ref_code_write policy pair. Row movement from ref_code_other to the new partition is automatic on Postgres 14+ (UPDATE domain=domain triggers tuple routing). Add to seeds_data.sql DDL block and to the manifest.

findings.title: ref_code_incident_category carries 4 RLS policies — two FOR ALL write policies are redundant duplicates
findings.evidence: pg_policy query returns: admin_ref_code_write (FOR ALL, standard ref_code pair) AND admin_incident_category_write (FOR ALL, identical predicate: auth.role() = ANY(['service_role','admin'])). Both are permissive FOR ALL on the same table. The duplicate is harmless for security (both allow the same principals) but violates the 'one per-command write policy family per table' invariant (CLAUDE.md write-policies rule).
findings.recommendation: DROP POLICY admin_incident_category_write ON ref_code_incident_category; — the standard admin_ref_code_write inherited from the ref_code family is sufficient. Apply inline in seeds_data.sql or as a targeted cleanup migration.

findings.title: 5 ops vocabulary domains (booking_status, client_type, partnership_type, insurance_type, feedback_type) have no business-table FK consumer and no FE usage — dead domains
findings.evidence: Full FK scan: the only FK targets for these 5 partition tables are object_taxonomy.domain (partition routing FK machinery) and ref_code_taxonomy_closure (closure table). No direct FK from any business entity table. Grep of bertel-tourism-ui/src returns zero matches for booking_status, client_type, partnership_type, insurance_type, feedback_type (beyond this code comment context). These were seeded as speculative 'Lot 1' data (seeds_data.sql lines 713-816) with no associated data model columns or API consumers yet built.
findings.recommendation: Classify as FORWARD-LOOKING — do not delete. Each has full en/es translations and will be needed when the corresponding modules are built (booking engine, CRM client profiling, partnership registry, insurance tracking, feedback collection). Document this status in the deferred-items tracker (WORKFLOW.md). No action required now; flag for revisit when each module is planned.

findings.title: crm_interaction.response_sentiment_id is NULL for all 3175 rows despite FK being defined
findings.evidence: SELECT count(*), count(response_sentiment_id) FROM crm_interaction returns total=3175, with_resp_sentiment=0. The column and FK (ref_code_crm_sentiment) exist but the import/save path never writes this field. The §61/§63 deferred list explicitly notes: 'response_sentiment_id (source NULL)'.
findings.recommendation: Already tracked in deferred list (WORKFLOW.md §61 CRM différés). No new action — confirm deferred status is documented. When the CRM interaction edit flow is built, wire response_sentiment_id to the crm_sentiment picker.

findings.title: demand_topic (20 codes), crm_sentiment (6 codes), distribution_channel (4 codes) have no en/es translations
findings.evidence: Initial live query: en=false, es=false for all rows in these three domains. seeds_data.sql line 1389 explicitly documents: 'les traductions des 11 demand_topic génériques + 22 demand_subtopic ont été [retirées] — sujets OTI du domaine demand_topic n'ont pas de traduction en/es (vocabulaire interne FR).' crm_sentiment and distribution_channel are similarly internal back-office French vocabularies.
findings.recommendation: No action required. These are internal back-office FR-only vocabularies used exclusively by OTI staff. en/es translations are not expected per §61 design decision. If the platform ever opens to multilingual staff, add translations then. Note once in WORKFLOW.md deferred list as 'by-design'.

findings.title: 3 empty partitions (demand_subtopic, incident_category, document_type) — correctly forward-looking, no gap
findings.evidence: SELECT count(*) from each partition table returns 0. No INSERT block exists for demand_subtopic or document_type in any seed or migration file. incident_category has no seed either. migration_crm_module.sql lines 21-23 explicitly preserve demand_subtopic as future. document_type is mentioned in a RAISE NOTICE (schema DDL era) but never seeded. incident_category is targeted by the deferred 'module demandes/board'.
findings.recommendation: No action. Document in WORKFLOW.md deferred tracker: demand_subtopic = subtopic vocabulary for future CRM topic hierarchy; incident_category = blocked on module demandes/board implementation; document_type = blocked on document upload by section (§59 deferred list).

findings.title: domain_registry is a separate table (ref_code_domain_registry), not a ref_code partition — scope classification artifact
findings.evidence: ref_code_domain_registry columns differ from ref_code (includes object_type, is_hierarchical, is_taxonomy — absent from ref_code). COUNT(*) FROM ref_code WHERE domain='domain_registry' = 0. COUNT(*) FROM ref_code_domain_registry = 19. FE accesses it directly (.from('ref_code_domain_registry')). Fully populated and consumed.
findings.recommendation: No data action. Remove domain_registry from future ref_code audit scope and maintain it in a separate 'registry tables' audit cluster. Confirm in audit index that ref_code_domain_registry is treated as a standalone registry, not a ref_code vocabulary partition.
